message:
fix(mvcc): forward btree native and wasm features explicitly

fsqlite-mvcc already forwarded its native and wasm feature selections into the
pager and WAL crates, but it stopped short of forwarding them into the B-tree
layer even though MVCC depends on B-tree behavior on both targets.

That omission leaves mvcc/native and mvcc/wasm with an incomplete feature graph:
consumers can select a target mode on MVCC and still end up with fsqlite-btree
configured differently from pager and WAL. In wasm builds that is especially
error-prone because the workspace deliberately disables default features in the
lower layers and relies on explicit propagation instead of accidental defaults.

Extend the mvcc feature definitions so:
- native also enables fsqlite-btree/native
- wasm also enables fsqlite-btree/wasm

This keeps the MVCC storage stack target-consistent from btree through pager
and WAL instead of letting B-tree drift onto a mismatched configuration.
